TextENGNew: When I heard the girls around me let out piercing screams, I finally snapped back to my senses.
Blood had splattered across the notebook on the desk, forming dark reddish-black stains.
changeReason: Old says blood 'dripped down on the notebook'; new says blood 'had splattered across the notebook.' 'Dripped' implies slow flow; 'splattered' implies violent dispersal. TextJPN 血が飛び散って means 'blood scattered/splattered.'

TextENGNew: At my feet, a girl was crouched down, clutching her arm.
I could hear her groaning in pain, but her face was hidden behind her hanging bangs.
Bright red blood kept spilling from beneath her clenched hand, steadily soaking through the white sleeve of her uniform.
changeReason: Old says blood was 'gently dyeing the sleeves'; new says blood 'kept spilling... steadily soaking through.' 'Gently' contradicts the urgency; TextJPN どんどんあふれ出して means 'kept overflowing/spilling more and more.'

TextENGNew: Two of her friends rushed over with pale faces to ask if she was okay,@k but whether from shock at the blood or sheer confusion at seeing it for the first time, she kept her head down and said nothing.
``S-Someone call the teacher! Hurry!!``
A boy—the class representative—bolted out of the classroom at those words.
changeReason: Old says 'A young boy dashed out'; new says 'A boy -- the class representative -- bolted out.' TextJPN specifies クラス委員の男子 (class representative), omitted in old translation.

TextENGNew: Watching him go, I... suddenly noticed the scissors that had fallen at my feet, and picked them up.
Then I held them out to the boy standing right beside me, frozen in shock.
They were his scissors.
I just wanted to give them back, that was all...
``...Ahh, aaaah?!``
The boy turned a face contorted with fear toward me and scrambled backward.
changeReason: Old says 'with a frightened expression and quickly backed up'; new says 'a face contorted with fear toward me and scrambled backward.' 'Contorted with fear' and 'scrambled' are more intense than 'frightened expression' and 'quickly backed up.'

TextENGNew: In his panic, he must not have noticed the desk and chair behind him... because he stumbled over them and crashed to the floor in spectacular fashion.
The sight was so absurdly clumsy and out of place that I couldn't help letting out a small laugh.
...But only then did I notice that the hand holding the scissors was smeared with blood.
I stared blankly at my own hands for a while, until gradually, color began returning to the white blankness of my mind.@k@|@y ...And then, all at once, my entire body started to tremble.
changeReason: Old says 'my head began to clear up'; new says 'color began returning to the white blankness of my mind.' TextJPN 真っ白になっていた頭に彩りが戻り means color returning to a mind that had gone blank-white -- the old misses the 'blank-white' state.

TextENGNew: ...Did I do this?
The one who inflicted this wound—enough to draw blood—on the girl moaning at my feet... was that me?
Then... the one who has frozen this entire classroom in an atmosphere of horror and disbelief... is me?!
As if to confirm it, my classmates kept their distance, their accusing, hostile gazes raining down on me.
changeReason: Old says classmates were 'glaring at me with hatred and blame'; new says they 'kept their distance, their accusing, hostile gazes raining down.' TextJPN specifies 距離を置いて (keeping distance), which old omits.

TextENGNew: ...You did this.
...What kind of person hurts a classmate like that?
Even without being spoken aloud, those words bypassed my ears entirely and struck straight into my mind.
changeReason: Old says 'Even if those words didn't reach my ears, they jumped straight into my head'; new says 'Even without being spoken aloud, those words bypassed my ears entirely and struck straight into my mind.' New clarifies the words weren't spoken aloud, matching TextJPN 音と響きを介さなくても.

TextENGNew: ``Th... that's not...!``
It wasn't me.
I didn't mean to... I never intended to hurt anyone.
I was just defending myself—I put just a little strength into pushing the hand away, just the tiniest bit...!
(Why...? Why am I the one being blamed?)
changeReason: Old says 'Why am I the bad guy here?'; new says 'Why am I the one being blamed?' TextJPN 私が悪いの means 'why am I at fault/in the wrong' -- 'being blamed' is closer to the passive sense than 'bad guy.'

TextENGNew: ...It was the boy who had been teasing me, trying to cut my hair with scissors.
changeReason: Old says 'It was the boy teasing me and trying to cut my hair'; new adds 'with scissors,' which connects to the scissors motif of the scene. TextJPN mentions ハサミ (scissors) in the broader context.

TextENGNew: If he hadn't brandished those scissors at me, none of this would have happened.
And the classmates who stood around watching it all with amusement, never once trying to stop it.
If even one of them had said 'stop it,' things never would have come to this.
And yet... why?
Why am I the one at fault?
Am I alone truly so wrong?!
changeReason: Old says 'I alone did something bad?!'; new says 'Am I alone truly so wrong?!' Old is a statement about action; new questions whether she herself is wrong. TextJPN 私一人がそんなに悪いことをしたの asks 'did I alone do something that wrong?'

TextENGNew: ``I-I...!@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2014. I was just...!``
Just then, a girl came rushing back with a first-aid kit, the school nurse close behind.
changeReason: Old says 'a girl with a first aid kit came rushing in, along with the health teacher'; new says 'a girl came rushing back with a first-aid kit, the school nurse close behind.' 'Health teacher' vs 'school nurse' and TextJPN 保健の先生 can mean either, but the character is labeled 保健医 (health doctor/nurse).

TextENGNew: She hurried to the injured girl and examined the wound on her arm, her face darkening.

``This is terrible... it'll need stitches!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hoken2002. Contact the homeroom teacher and call an ambulance!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hoken2003. You there, help stop the bleeding!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_hoken2004. ...?!``
Then the nurse noticed me standing nearby... and the moment she saw the blood-stained scissors in my hand, she fixed me with a sharp, condemning stare.
changeReason: Old says 'the teacher noticed me'; new says 'the nurse noticed me' -- consistent with 8297's change from 'health teacher' to 'school nurse,' matching the character label 保健医.

TextENGNew: ...You're the one who did this to her?
How cruel. How violent...!
Those unspoken words, carried on the cold light of her eyes, made my earlobes tremble.
changeReason: Old says 'The cold gaze transmitted that message to my ears'; new says 'Those unspoken words, carried on the cold light of her eyes, made my earlobes tremble.' TextJPN 耳朶を震わせる literally means 'made my earlobes tremble,' missed in old.

TextENGNew: ...That's not true.
It wasn't me.
It wasn't me!
I'm not the one at fault!
changeReason: Old says 'I'm not the one who did something wrong!'; new says 'I'm not the one at fault!' TextJPN 私悪くない means 'I'm not bad/at fault' -- more about blame than action.

TextENGNew: Someone, listen!
Listen to meeeee!!!
